hints.pannis: 'pannis' is a noun meaning 'swaddling clothes' in the ablative

hints.to involve: English 'involve' evolved in meaning — Latin 'involvere' literally means 'to wrap/roll in'

hints.Present: Present 'involvit' looks identical but the narrative context confirms perfect tense
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect would be 'involvebat' — 'involvit' is 3rd singular perfect
hints.Future: Future would be 'involvet' — 'involvit' is 3rd singular perfect

hints.volvo, volvere, volvi, volutum: These are the parts of 'volvere' (to roll) — 'involvere' adds the prefix in-
hints.solvo, solvere, solvi, solutum: These are the parts of 'solvere' (to loosen/release)
hints.evolvo, evolvere, evolvi, evolutum: These are the parts of 'evolvere' (to unroll) — different prefix

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'panni' — 'pannis' is dative/ablative plural
hints.Dative: 'pannis' could be dative plural, but here it is ablative of means (wrapped with cloths)
hints.Accusative: Accusative plural would be 'pannos' — 'pannis' is ablative plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'panni' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'panni' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'panni' ends in -i

translation: And she wrapped him in swaddling clothes
